title: datacenter_fire
description: Smoke detectors activate in the datacenter after a server-rack electrical fault ignites.

timeline.title: Smoke Detected
timeline.description: Environmental sensors detect smoke in the main server room. Fire alarm activates.

timeline.title: Evacuation of Datacenter
timeline.description: Datacenter personnel evacuate. Access restricted pending fire service assessment.

timeline.title: Fire Service Arrival
timeline.description: Fire service confirms a localised rack fire. Suppression system activated.

timeline.title: Power Isolation
timeline.description: Emergency power-off triggered to prevent further electrical ignition.

timeline.title: Damage Assessment
timeline.description: Fire extinguished. Damage limited to two racks. Residual suppression agent requires clean-up.

timeline.title: Failover and Recovery
timeline.description: Workloads failing over to DR site. Physical damage documented. Clean-up and restoration begins.

node-1.title: Datacenter Fire Alarm Activated
node-1.situation: Smoke detectors in the main server room have triggered. The fire alarm is sounding. Two datacenter engineers are currently working inside the room. Visible smoke is coming from a server rack in row 3.
node-1.question: What is your immediate action?

options.label: A
options.text: Immediately evacuate all personnel from the datacenter, do not attempt to fight the fire manually, seal the room, and contact the fire service. Treat it as a real fire until confirmed otherwise.

options.label: B
options.text: Ask the engineers inside to investigate the smoke source and attempt to use a portable extinguisher on the rack.

options.label: C
options.text: Reset the alarm — server racks occasionally produce smoke without a real fire.

node-2.title: Fire Service Response
node-2.situation: All personnel have evacuated. The fire service has arrived and confirmed a localised electrical fire in two adjacent rack units in row 3. The automated gas suppression system has activated. The engineers want to re-enter to shut down servers before they overheat from loss of cooling.
node-2.question: Do you allow re-entry to perform server shutdowns?

options.label: A
options.text: Do not allow re-entry until the fire service has formally cleared the room. Instead, initiate remote graceful shutdown of at-risk systems from a safe external console if available.

options.label: B
options.text: Allow the engineers to re-enter wearing office clothing — the fire service says the main fire is out.

node-3.title: Emergency Power-Off Decision
node-3.situation: The fire service recommends triggering the Emergency Power-Off (EPO) system to cut all electrical supply to the room and prevent re-ignition. This will cause an unclean shutdown of all remaining running servers. Some systems have unsaved transactions.
node-3.question: Do you activate the EPO?

options.label: A
options.text: Activate the EPO on the advice of the fire service — life safety and preventing re-ignition takes priority over data integrity. Data recovery can be attempted from transaction logs after the incident.

options.label: B
options.text: Refuse to activate the EPO until all transaction data can be flushed — the fire appears contained.

node-4.title: Damage Assessment and DR Activation
node-4.situation: The fire is extinguished. The fire service has cleared the room for a damage assessment. Two full racks are destroyed. Suppression agent residue covers affected equipment. The DR site BCP requires activation. Business leadership is asking for an RTO estimate.
node-4.question: How do you manage the recovery?

options.label: A
options.text: Formally declare a disaster, activate the DR site failover for affected systems, provide a realistic RTO based on the DR plan, and begin a documented damage assessment for insurance and root-cause purposes.

options.label: B
options.text: Attempt to recover the damaged servers in-place before activating DR to avoid the complexity of a failover.

node-5.title: Scenario Complete
node-5.situation: The DR site has successfully taken over affected workloads within the agreed RTO. All personnel are safe. The damaged racks have been documented and the insurance claim initiated. A post-incident review has been scheduled to assess the fire detection, suppression, and EPO systems.
node-5.question: Scenario complete. Well done!
